intended_use: Second Opinion® 3D is a radiological automated image processing software device intended to identify and mark clinically relevant anatomy in dental CBCT radiographs; specifically Dentition, Maxilla, M
intended_use_full: Second Opinion® 3D is a radiological automated image processing software device intended to identify and mark clinically relevant anatomy in dental CBCT radiographs; specifically Dentition, Maxilla, Mandible, Inferior Alveolar Canal and Mental Foramen (IAN), Maxillary Sinus, Nasal space, and airway. It should not be used in lieu of full patient evaluation or solely relied upon to make or confirm a diagnosis. It is designed to aid health professionals to review CBCT radiographs of patients 12 years of age or older as a concurrent and second reader.
target_condition: Dental anatomical visualization in CBCT radiographs
ground_truth: Not explicitly stated in the document
other_metrics: Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) reported for seven anatomies: Dentition 0.86 (95% CI 0.83-0.89); Maxillary Complex 0.91 (95% CI 0.91-0.92); Mandible 0.97 (95% CI 0.97-0.97); IAN Canal 0.76 (95% CI 0.74-0.78); Maxillary Sinus 0.97 (95% CI 0.97-0.98); Nasal Space 0.90 (95% CI 0.89-0.91); Airway 0.95 (95% CI 0.94-0.96). All p-values < 0.000001. Predicate device (Relu Creator) reported Dice score 0.92±0.02 for dental implant segmentation.
